symptoms.symptom_name: Abrupt behavioural escalation immediately after a minor routine change, with no preceding trauma

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute behavioural dysregulation crisis of FASD
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A trivial routine disruption triggering catastrophic dysregulation is characteristic of the executive dysfunction of FASD.
A clear environmental trigger with no fall or knock makes a traumatic cause less likely, though not excluded on history alone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Repeated danger-blind attempts to run out onto a busy road

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute behavioural dysregulation crisis of FASD with imminent elopement risk
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Impaired impulse control and absent safety awareness produce relentless elopement — the immediate life threat in this case.

symptoms.symptom_name: Physical aggression the exhausted sole carer cannot contain

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute behavioural dysregulation crisis of FASD with imminent elopement risk
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of containment by the sole carer converts a behavioural crisis into a safety emergency requiring in-person help.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile, no ear/jaw pain, no rash or injury, no preceding head knock

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Occult pain or infection driving agitation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Negative screening on available history lowers the probability of a treatable organic driver but cannot exclude it without an in-person exam.
No fall/knock and no focal neurology on the carer's account reduce the likelihood of a traumatic cause.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute behavioural dysregulation crisis of FASD
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sympathetic arousal from extreme agitation and exertion; non-specific and cannot distinguish behavioural distress from a medical driver — clinician-measured, sealed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Occult pain or infection driving agitation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever lowers the likelihood of an intercurrent infection as the driver — clinician-measured, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Microcephaly, pre- and post-natal growth retardation, characteristic facial features (smooth philtrum, thin vermilion border, short palpebral fissures)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute behavioural dysregulation crisis of FASD
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The stigmata confirm the FASD baseline; clinician-observed and sealed, not something the AI Doctor measures.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pupils equal and reactive, no focal motor deficit, clear chest, soft abdomen

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring in-person neurological and systemic exam lowers the probability of head injury or acute physical illness — sealed clinician exam.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regular morning guanfacine given; as-needed melatonin and usual calming strategies ineffective for the acute episode

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute behavioural dysregulation crisis of FASD with imminent elopement risk
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Failure of maintenance therapy and usual strategies to abort the episode marks the severity of the acute crisis.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute behavioural dysregulation crisis of FASD with imminent elopement risk
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Relentless elopement toward traffic plus aggression the sole carer cannot contain is the combination that makes this a 000 emergency, not a routine behavioural problem.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person clinical screen for an organic driver (temperature, ears/throat/teeth, abdomen, skin, examination for injury)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A new acute behaviour change in a child with a disability must be examined for a treatable medical cause; this can only be done in person, by responders or at the in-person review.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Assessment for possible accidental ingestion (with Poisons Information 13 11 26 as needed)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The chaotic environment raises the possibility of ingestion; must be considered and excluded in person.
No history of trauma and no focal neurology — routine neuroimaging is not indicated and would be over-investigation; reserve only for new focal signs or a confirmed significant head injury.
